articles.title: Niv-AI exits stealth to wring more power performance out of GPUs
articles.summary: The company raised $12 million in seed funding to measure and manage GPU power surges.

articles.title: Nurturing agentic AI beyond the toddler stage
articles.summary: Parents of young children face a lot of fears about developmental milestones, from infancy through adulthood. The number of months it takes a baby to learn to talk or walk is often used as a benchmark for wellness, or an indicator of additional tests needed to properly diagnose a potential health condition. A parent rejoices…

articles.title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
articles.summary: Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.

articles.title: Nvidia’s version of OpenClaw could solve its biggest problem: security
articles.summary: Nvidia announced an open enterprise AI agent platform, called NemoClaw, that is built off of viral OpenClaw.

articles.title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote — and what to expect
articles.summary: GTC is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically announces new products, partnerships, and its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role in the future of computing and AI.

articles.title: Another deep tech chip startup becomes a unicorn: Frore hits $1.64B
articles.summary: At Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's urging, Frore developed liquid-cooling tech for chips. That shift helped it raise $143 million.

articles.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
articles.summary: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

articles.title: OpenAI sidesteps Nvidia with unusually fast coding model on plate-sized chips
articles.summary: OpenAI's new GPT‑5.3‑Codex‑Spark is 15 times faster at coding than its predecessor.

articles.title: Jensen Huang just put Nvidia’s Blackwell and Vera Rubin sales projections into the $1 trillion stratosphere
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said he expects $1 trillion worth of orders for the chips.

articles.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 uses generative AI to boost photorealism in video games, with ambitions beyond gaming
articles.summary: Nvidia’s new DLSS 5 uses generative AI and structured graphics data to make video games more realistic. CEO Jensen Huang says the approach could eventually spread to other industries.

articles.title: Google Maps Gets Chatty With a New Gemini-Powered Interface
articles.summary: “Ask Maps,” rolling out today to Google Maps on mobile, lets you ask Gemini questions about locations and even to plan trips on your behalf.

articles.title: The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
articles.summary: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has learned.  AI models like Anthropic’s Claude are already used to answer questions in classified settings; applications include analyzing targets in Iran. But allowing models to train on…

articles.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here.
